I'll bring you everything we have on them, but it's not much.
So don't get your hopes up. It's mostly lies, leavened with rumor and conjecture.
The ice entombs the heart. The bane of Kagrenac and Dagoth Ur.
To harness it is to know. The fundaments. The Dwemer lockbox hides it from me.
The Elder Scroll gives insight deeper than the deep ones, though. To bring about the opening.
Somewhere up north, in the ice fields.
Said he found some old Dwemer artifact, but... well, that was years ago.
Haven't heard from him since.
Oh no. I hope not. But even I haven't seen him in years, and we were close.
Became obsessed with the Dwemer. Took off north saying he had found some old artifact. Haven't seen him since.
Somewhere in the ice fields, if you want to try to find him.
As you desire.
(Heavy breathing)
(Heavy breathing)
(Heavy breathing)
Got a pretty full stock of potions and alchemy reagents.
Nothing, until you choose to use it.
Then, well... you got a taste. Stronger, faster.
It doesn't last long, though. The blood of your foes can sustain you. If you're willing to feed.
Do you?
It is true there are some here who have spent years studying the accumulated knowledge of the scrolls.
But what you seek does not come easily, and can destroy those without a strong will.
I will see you dead, monster.
What about... wait. Are you? Were you the one the Greybeards were calling?
And what do you plan to do with it? Do you even know what you're asking about, or are you just someone's errand girl?
And what do you plan to do with it? Do you even know what you're asking about?
Werewolf!
It's a werewolf! To arms! To arms!
We're done with this when none of them are left standing. Let's go.
And now... I think I can rest. Farewell, my friend, wherever your travels take you.
So, the beast is destroyed.
Not only that, but Auriel's Bow is in safe hands.
The Dawnguard will now be dedicated to safeguarding it, making sure that prophecy will never come to pass.
You've served Skyrim well.
Even with these vampires gone, the fight isn't completely over. Once we're settled back in at the fort, there will be more work to do.
We'd be honored to have you join us.
Vampires need to be dealt with. Hunted, rooted out, destroyed.
Once there was a group dedicated to a similar cause. They called themselves the Dawnguard.
That was a long time ago, but the idea was, and is, a good one. That's why I'm bringing it back.
This latest attack proves it's the right time.
Well, maybe it really is time to resurrect the Dawnguard.
Well it was... Thank the Vigilants for ruining that.
Ugh. I thought this place was supposed to be secret?
Hold it! He's the one Tolan mentioned.
Hold it! She's the one Tolan mentioned.
Here comes another one!
Arvak! Arvak, where are you? Arvak, please come back! Come back!
Please, he's such a loyal creature, and he's been running for so long. You have to save him! A place like this will change you...
Arvak. My horse. We came to this horrible place together. We were attacked by monsters, so I told him to run.
You must help me find my Arvak. He doesn't deserve to be in a place like this!
The only way to get to my brother is by following in the Initiates' footsteps and traveling from wayshrine to wayshrine just as they did.
The first lay at the end of Darkfall Passage, a cavern that represents the absence of enlightenment.
I'm afraid in their current form, they still believe the Chantry to be an active center of worship.
They won't respond to you in any way other than believing that you're an Initiate and you're undertaking the journey to the Inner Sanctum.
They're ghosts of the snow elf priests that tended the wayshrines before being slaughtered by the Betrayed.
Through the grace of Auri-El, they were restored to their spectral form to enable them to continue their duties.
So, let's get this straight.
We need to do all that nonsense to get into the temple, so we can kill your brother and claim Auriel's Bow?
Yes, let me show you.
They swept into the Chantry without warning and began killing everyone without pause.
Of course. You're here for Auriel's Bow. Why else would you be here?
I can help you get it, but first I must have your assistance.
Because that means there's still hope that they might one day shed their hatred and learn to believe in Auri-El once again.
It's been a long time since I felt that way and it's been long overdue. My thanks, to both of you.
A vampire?
I see. That would explain much.
Deep inside, it brings me joy that the Betrayed weren't to blame for what happened here.
I did. But something about you makes me think I can trust you. I hope I'm not wrong.
Anyway, my mother and I didn't feel like inviting a war with all of Tamriel, so we tried to stop him. That's why I was sealed away with the Scroll.
He just became absorbed... obsessed. It was kind of sick, actually.
The prophecy said that vampires would no longer need to fear the sun. For someone who fancied himself as vampire royalty, that's pretty seductive.
I wonder what these souls do all day. They look... bored.
Be careful with those crystals. They feel... hungry.
Another one of those things. Let's keep our distance.
Ugh. The smell here... let's try to handle this quickly.
This better not be all the things you just can't be bothered with.
Anything good?
If you think it'll help.
All right, let's see what you have.
Why don't you keep your things, and I'll keep mine.
I think I'll hang on to my things, thank you.
Hands to yourself.
Not going to happen.
My congratulations on defeating Harkon. Clearly, you are the superior vampire.
You are the new Master; we bow to your power. A power which, I note, includes Auriel's Bow.
The castle, of course, is yours. We shall follow you, and help spread your influence across Skyrim.
Run, coward! Run back to the filthy Dawnguard!
Run, coward! You're a traitor to your race!
When I find you, I'm going to gut you like a deer and feast upon you blood!
Enough games, let's finish this!
It ends here!
I'll rend the flesh from your bones!
I will darken the skies!
Auriel's Bow will be mine!
What trickery is this?
Hiding won't help you!
No... Serana... your own father....
Die, mortal!
Die, traitor!
Your blood will be mine!
I'll drain the life from you!
I should have killed you and your mother long ago!
I'll make you pay for this, daughter!
You dare defy your father?!
First you die, then Serana suffers!
Serana isn't yours, she's mine!
You've turned her against me!
Die, you traitorous dog!
How dare you disobey me!
You'll pay for your insolence!
You can't stop the prophecy!
I'll rip the Dawnguard apart!
I despise your presence, mortal!
May Auri-El keep you free from harm.
May Auri-El watch over you.
May Auri-El light your path.
Look, take whatever you want. I'm getting out of here!
Help me!
I'm getting out of here!
Gods, Vampires! I... I can't take this anymore! I'm not cut out for this!
Stand aside!
Forgive me, but I am on urgent business for the Dawnguard. I have no time to speak with you.
Stand aside!
Forgive me, but I am on urgent business for the Dawnguard. Stand aside!
Halt, vampire!
Your end has come!
Light take you!
Death to the Dawnguard!
Perish, worm!
Shadows take you!
That's close enough, fool. Now you die!
You there! Traveler! Over here!
Well met, friend. We've been chasing that fiend for a long time. On behalf of the Vigil, you have my thanks.
It's done, then. We've been chasing that fiend for a long time.
I suspect now it was Brother Adalvald's journals they were after. He was obsessed with ancient vampire artifacts and burial sites.
My name is Serana. Were you... sent to wake me?
Who sent you?
Are you sure? I'm willing to turn you, but you need to think it through.
You'd become the very thing you've sworn to destroy. I don't know how the Dawnguard will react.
I'm sorry. I wish I knew a better way, something that would be easier for you.
Just know that... whatever path you choose, I won't think any less of you. Sometimes things just have to be done.
I know that better than anybody.
Maybe.
We could just "pay the toll" another way. It wants a soul, so we give it a soul. Yours.
Not your first choice, I'd guess.
There might be, but I don't think you're going to like it.
Vampires aren't counted among the living. I could probably go through there without a problem.
Now that I think about it... I should have expected that. Sorry.
It's hard to describe. The Soul Cairn is... well, hungry, for lack of a better word. It's trying to take your life essence as payment.
Are you alright? That looked painful.
Only what I've heard.
Back in the Second Era, the Jarl of Riften had a son. Adventurous type, too much for his own good it seems.
Wound up turning into a vampire. The Jarl, unable to kill his own son, spent a fortune building this keep and hiring men to guard it.
Their job was to keep the Jarl's son contained within. They took it seriously, and served honorably.
They were eventually forced to put him down. The Jarl banished them from the hold, but they stuck to their cause.
Fought vampires wherever they found them. I aim to continue that mission.
A basic one? Of course. That is, assuming you know anything about steel smithing. It's pretty simple.
Talk to Gunmar if you need materials.
Someone is here. Find them!
I will find you. Then you will beg me to let you die.
Harkon will reward us richly for this.
I've waited a long time for my chance to move up in the court.
I thought Lokil knew what he was doing. This is taking too long.
Jeron was furious to be left guarding the door.
Arvak!
Arvak, please! You can stop running now!
Arvak?
My mother was meticulous about her research. If we can find her notes, there might be some hints in there.
Let's take a look around. There has to be something here that tells us where she's gone.
I'm not sure about this circle, but it's obviously... something.
I knew she was deep into necromancy. I mean, she taught me everything I know.
But I had no idea she had a setup like this.
So be it!
Be still!
Then you will be prey, like all mortals. I will spare your life this once, but you will be banished from this hall.
There is but one gift I can give that is equal in value to the Elder Scroll and my daughter.
I offer you my blood. Take it, and you will walk as a lion among sheep. Men will tremble at your approach, and you will never fear death again.
Not just vampires. We are among the oldest and most powerful vampires in Skyrim.
I am Harkon, lord of this court.
By now, my daughter will have told you what we are.
Look at this place. This has to be it!
Let's go. My mother must be waiting on the other side of that thing.
Have you made up your mind?
Incredible. Simply incredible.
Get the ingredients in the vessel and let me know when you're ready.
Having trouble finding those ingredients?
Any luck yet?
Keep searching for her notes. She probably had a journal or something.
I can't even begin to imagine how much time she spent in this room. Doesn't look too cozy.
We're getting close. I'm sure of it.
If we had the crests, we wouldn't have to keep hanging around this lovely place.
I'm telling you, there's something strange with the moondial.
Yes? What did you need?
True. But I know a way we can get to the courtyard without arousing suspicion.
Trust me. I lived here for a very long time and I know every nook and cranny.
Trust me. I lived in that castle for a very long time and I know every nook and cranny.
They'd be even less welcoming to her than they've been to me. It would have been a bloodbath.
And since the Dawnguard are still around, that must not have happened.
Any other ideas?
I doubt my mother would waste her time with those fools.
They probably would have tried to kill her, and that has a way of souring relationships.
Any other ideas?
Besides, I can't imagine a single place my father would avoid looking. And he's had all this time, too.
Any ideas?
Maybe.
What I can't figure out is why she said it that way.
The last time I saw her, she said that she'd go somewhere safe... somewhere that my father would never search.
Other than that, she wouldn't tell me anything.
But the way she said it... "someplace he would never search." It was cryptic, yet she called attention to it.
It's not a question of trust... he's just obsessed with the prophecy.
And from what I could tell, a thousand extra years of obsession haven't made him any better. We should have found him a hobby.
Didn't want that lot getting wind of it. Especially my father.
Half the people in your little crew would just as soon kill me as talk to me.
That doesn't exactly make me want to open up.
I got a warmer welcome from my father, and that's saying something.
Do you have a moment to talk?
Yesa?| mastera?|
Yes, I can feel your defenses crumbling. You want it to end. You want to give in to me.
Now, acknowledge me as your master!
Must... resist...
How much longer can you keep this up, Moth Priest?
Your mind was strong, but you're exhausted from the struggle.
I will resist you, monster. I must!
The more you fight me, the more you will suffer, mortal.
I'm working on it, now give me some silence so I can think.
How do we do that?
I don't know, maybe. Won't know until we get him out of there.
You think they turned him?
I just hope we weren't too late.
I'm not sure what they did to this Moth Priest, but he doesn't look right.
Those vampires put up a hell of a fight.
Farewell, and good journey milord.
Farewell, and good journey milady.
Thank you for breaking that foul vampire's hold over me.
That... that wasn't me you were fighting.
I could see through my eyes, but I could not control my actions.
Wait, stop! I yield!
Are you alright? Come on, we can do this. I know we can.
Yes, they smell like trolls. But do you have to? Consider washing up a bit more often.
They're trolls. They smell like trolls. I can't help that either.
I'd also like to not have to smell troll all the time.
I can't help that space is cramped in here, Florentius.
I'd like to be able to make a potion without tasting iron flakes, you know?
Very funny.
I don't know how you do it, getting them into that pen and putting armor on them. A very special relationship, clearly.
What?
Why the trolls, of course! You seem to have quite a way with them.
Which friends would those be?
How are your friends?
I've only heard stories. Not sure if any of them are true.
Like that they were supposed to guard some vampire here, but wound up all becoming vampires themselves when they saw how powerful he was.
And then preyed on the Rift until finally someone put them down.
I guess it doesn't matter now. At least Isran is trying to do something noble with the name.
Oh, I've read a few things. Heard a few more things. Arkay's told me a bit as well.
The story they'd like you to believe is that they were such a noble bunch, doing the honorable thing and saving lives.
Only I hear that's not quite how it happened. Secret organization, you see. No one to blame when homes are broken into, things stolen.
Heard they'd go on midnight raids, pillaging and looting, and then stash the spoils somewhere, guarded by their sigil.
Arkay tells me the Sun Fire spell can break open those caches, but I've never found one. I think he might be lying to me.
Sorine and Gunmar are settling in.
Glad to see you've taken care of your little problem, and kept it from becoming my problem.
Welcome back.
I'll be waiting.
If you can manage that, the Dawnguard still may have use for you.
Go to Morthal and find Falion. He's the only one I know of in Skyrim that can cure vampirism.
And so I'm going to give you a chance to get yourself cured.
Since you could've attacked me before now, I'm going to assume this was a little accident on your part.
And it seems we have a problem.
Well, I think it's time I got back to my work as an alchemist.
The Soul Cairn will offer a unique opportunity to continue my studies, and I intend to complete my research.
In the meantime, if there are any potions you might need, feel free to help yourself.
I'm sorry, I don't feel comfortable giving them to you.
I understand what you did for Serana and for me, but the prophecy never sat well for me, and I don't want to be a part of it. I'm sorry.
Oh, gods...
That many fewer vampires to worry about. Good work.
Isran will never admit it, but I'm sure he's proud of you.
Good thing we stopped that prophecy, huh?
I really do enjoy killing vampires.
Another victory for the Dawnguard.
Harkon's short-sightedness was his undoing. In his lust for power, he failed to consider the full consequences of his plans.
Can you imagine it? A world without a sun... we would all have been doomed, vampires and men alike.
Once again, the Elder Scrolls have proven their value.
Congratulations, friend. You've won a well-deserved victory here.
The people of Skyrim owe you a great deal, whether they know it or not.
Shall we continue the fight?
I knew you had it in you! Arkay wasn't so sure. Between you and me, I think he didn't expect you to make it.
But not me! I knew it all along!
Is that what passes for security with your lot? I can't say that I'm feeling any more confident.
I don't want to either, but you're going to have to jump. I'll be... right behind you. Above you. Just go.
Is that... oh no. Are we going down there?
Oh. All right, then.
I think you'll need to find some arrows, first. Only elven ones will work for this, remember?
Let's just get this over with.
Just... try to make it quick.
So I am. I guess we could use my blood. Do you have any arrows? Elven arrows. I'm not going to just bleed on any old thing.
That I am. So I'm guessing you want to just use my blood, then?
Let's just get this over with. Do you have any elven arrows?
If you were anybody else, I'd probably be taking some blood from you for asking that.
You wouldn't like it.
But I can see this having its uses. Do you have any elven arrows on you?
Sure... do you have any arrows, though? They would have to be of elven quality, I'd guess.
It sounds like... that's the last bit of the prophecy. The blood of a Daughter of Coldharbour can corrupt the bow.
(Growl)
(Bark)
(Panting)
(Bark)
(Bark)
(Whine)
(Bark)
(Whine)
Cyrodiil is the seat of an empire?
I must have been gone longer than I thought. Definitely longer than we planned.
Please, let's hurry. I need to get home so I can figure out what's happened.
Empire? What... what empire?
Look out! Gargoyles!
Well I don't know what that means. But we need to find out. She must either have something the vampires want, or be someone important to them.
I don't know that name. You say she's Jarl of Solitude? Who supports her?
I've... never heard of him. Is he well supported?
Oh, wonderful. A war of succession. Good to know the world didn't get boring while I was gone.
Who are the contenders?
I wonder if the horse's remains are around here... someplace.
That's it. That's the last scroll.
Come on, let's take it to Dexion.
Suit yourself.
You know where to find me if you change your mind.
That's what I wanted to hear.
If you need some help, I'm ready.
Of course, if you've got any more adventures planned...
I'll probably stay here, for as long as they'll let me.
I think we can rebuild here. Make my family's legacy something more respectable.
I'll probably stay with the Dawnguard, for as long as they'll let me.
They're respectable fighters, and I think they see the benefits of having a vampire on their side, now.
I'm not sure.
Anyway, this is all very touching, but don't we have some more important things to worry about right now?
Well, I'll be out of your hair soon enough.
Me too.
That's too bad.
You deserve to be happy.
Good. You deserve that much.
Well, I guess now you have one more.
Does it bother you that I'm here?
Oh. I didn't even know. What kind of woman is she?
Oh. I didn't even know. What kind of man is he?
Oh. I didn't even know. Is it a happy home?
Yeah. Is it just you, or do you have... people in your life?
Do you get lonely?
Are there... people in your life?
But... what about you?
No, they didn't. But that's why I like being out here with you.
A little bit. That's... one of the reasons I wanted to come with you.
Well, growing up the way I did, you get used to it.
Do you ever feel lonely?
Come on, the bow has to be in this valley somewhere.
We've got enough to worry about right now. You stay focused, and I'll worry about me.
I know you didn't know your parents. But if they were anything like mine, you're better off.
I think both of our parents have failed us. Just you and me against the world, now.
We didn't all get to have parents like yours. Sometimes this is just... how it is.
I hope so.
I don't think you can ever be ready to kill your parent.
I'm doing my best to not think of him as my father anymore.
Just... leave me alone for now. I'll be fine.
Come on. We can talk about this another time.
You... always say what you think, don't you?
I don't like him any more than you do. But he is my father.
I'm getting there. Making my peace with it.
If? I've been assuming that's where all this is going.
I've been trying to make my peace with it.
Not until we have the bow. But... you're right.
This won't be easy. But we can handle it.
I think you know.
We both know what's going to have to happen once we find the bow.
Keep strong, and eyes open. We're on a different path now.
You'll have shown yourself to be strong, which means you can't be allowed to just stick around.
And I'll be unnecessary once he has it.
No, you've proven your strength to him.
Which just makes you a more dangerous rival.
He won't need either of us anymore.
And I thought my father was the delusional one.
At that point, you'll have given him everything he's ever wanted.
Of course he will.
But at that point, he'll have everything he's ever wanted.
No, it's probably not.
I mean, what do you think my father will do when we bring him the bow?
Don't tell me you're that naive.
Really?
Oh, wow. All right, well, what do you think my father's going to do once we bring the bow to him?
I can't say it surprises me. I kind of figured we were heading for this some day.
I just didn't know when.
I figured it would be more of an issue for you.
That makes sense.
Oh. I... I see.
It is.
Not as much.
Yeah.
Let's... uh... let's just keep going.
Right behind you.
You're a good son.
You're a good daughter.
I know how that feels. I mean... I know it isn't the same thing. But I was a pretty lonely child, myself.
That's too bad. Do you know how they're doing now?
Oh, I'm sorry. Didn't mean to bring up a bad memory.
What about you? What were your parents like?
It's not that simple. I guess it never is with families, is it?
My father... no, not really. I did spend a lot of time with my mother, but she saw me more like a protege than a daughter.
You've met both of my parents. They're both crazy in their own way.
You've already seen my father's obsession. My mother's not a whole lot better, but you'll see that soon enough.
There's not a whole lot to tell.
Very supportive.
I understand.
I'll speak to Falion. I've heard he knows about these things.
I'm going to go away for a while. I'll meet you back home when I'm done.
So you take a major decision for me, and make it all about you?
Forget it.
It would be nice not to always be so thirsty.
Even before that, we weren't the most normal family. We did worship a daedra, remember?
Of course it matters to you, but you don't get to make this decision for me.
I can see that. This has been my life for a long time, though. Do you understand that much?
Probably not.
I can tell this is important to you. Would you... do you want me to be just a normal human?
It's fine, it can just be kind of a sore subject.
Stop apologizing for everything.
I'm not talking about this with you any more.
You brought that up before... I think I was a little short with you.
No... why?
That makes sense.
Figured.
You're all talk.
Don't say I didn't warn you.
All right, then. Hold still.
I don't think that's something you want. You're a little... I just don't think you're the type.
Are you sure that's something you want? This isn't like picking out a new outfit. You'll be a creature of the night, like me.
It's not like anything else in Skyrim, I can tell you that much. From now or... before.
There's probably groves like this all over Tamriel. Most people just don't even know what to look for.
And I thought the Soul Cairn was creepy.
I wonder how long they've been like this.
These Falmer are... they're frozen in the ice.
Wait, is he talking about me?
"The Winking Skeever." Charming name.
You're going to go kill a Dawnguard spy.
But don't draw attention. Some kills are for fun, some for profit. And others are for sending messages. This one is a message.
With any luck he'll bleed to death before anyone notices. A cold corpse will say what we want better than a warm one.
I've learned the whereabouts of a troublesome Dawnguard member. You will track him down and dispose of him.
But please, do be discreet. Our aim isn't to stir the nest, just to skin a rat, reminding them that they are the prey.
Apparently you had blood in your ears when I said not to draw attention to yourself. If I wanted a messy kill I would have said so.
But a kill is a kill, and it'll send a message to the Dawnguard, if not exactly the one we wanted.
While that's one less trouble maker to keep an eye on, you could have been more discreet in your approach to the problem.
I had thought better of you.
Well done. Nothing like a good kill to get the blood pumping, is there?
Excellent. I knew I could count on you. That's one less trouble maker to keep an eye on.
Here's where I fell. Feels like ages ago.
Watch your step.
Not bad. Not bad at all.
Look, there's a chest in there. Door's sealed tight, though...
Falmer up ahead.
Watch yourself. Since the quake, this place has been pretty unstable.
Hey! Grab my journal. If you survive, you're going to need it.
All right. Good luck.
It's your funeral.
Careful now... this area's not stable.
Watch yourself.
That was close...
See that thing?
I spent my life tracking down that legend... until my damn apprentice stole everything from me, and published it all under his own name.
And now... now I can't rest. Not until I have proof. Something to show the world what I discovered, what I did. That my life... mattered.
It's a long story.
All right. Lead on, then.
Watch out!
You ever heard of Aetherium? A rare mineral used by the dwarves.
Supposedly, they had to build a special forge, the Aetherium Forge, to even work with it.
The items it made were so powerful they went to war over it, and the Forge was lost. Or so the story goes.
You know what this is? It's a lock. A 'Tonal Lock'. Simple, and very, very deadly.
Really? Well, now, that make things easier...
(Sigh)...
Bother someone else.
You realize it's not really necessary to take on that form here.
Well met, sister.
Hello there.
Do you need something?
I am at your service, as always.
Life here is better than you could find anywhere in Skyrim.
Remember your place, and you'll be taken care of.
I hope you find Lord Harkon's court to your liking.
Here we are, in view of seeing the prophecy fulfilled. These are truly great times.
You realize that doesn't impress anyone here.
Don't get in my way, and I won't tear your arms off. Simple, isn't it?
You can't trust a word that comes out of Vingalmo's mouth. Remember that.
We need strength, power. Not magic. Elves don't seem to understand that.
So you found the Moth Priest. Let's see you find the rest of the Elder Scrolls all on your own.
You've gone from a between-meal snack to the darling of the court. Impressive.
I don't converse with my meals.
None can deny your power now.
I wish Orthjolf and Vingalmo would just get it over with and kill each other. I'm tired of listening to them.

This "stranger" has done more for me in the brief time I've known her than you've done in centuries!

This stranger aligns herself with those that would hunt you down and slay you like an animal, yet I should entrust you to her?

A pathetic creature in her present condition, but the gift will embolden her. And her lowly state suits her to being our eyes and ears in Markarth.

Put his mind to rest, allay his fear through vampiric seduction and when he is in a pliant condition, embrace him and bite him on the neck.
Put her mind to rest, allay her fear through vampiric seduction and when she is in a pliant condition, embrace her and bite her on the neck.

(grunt)
(grunt)

Not a serious threat, but we can't leave them completely unchecked or they may become one.
